records.ety: [AS. spendan (in comp.), fr. L. expendere or dispendere to weigh out, to expend, dispense. See Pendant, and cf. Dispend, Expend, Spence, Spencer.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Spent (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Spending.]

defs: To weigh or lay out; to dispose of; to part with; as, to spend money for clothing.
quotes: Spend thou that in the town.
Wherefore do ye spend money for that which is not bread?

defs: To bestow; to employ; -- often with on or upon.
quotes: I . . . am never loath To spend my judgment.

defs: To consume; to waste; to squander; to exhaust; as, to spend an estate in gaming or other vices.
To pass, as time; to suffer to pass away; as, to spend a day idly; to spend winter abroad.
quotes: We spend our years as a tale that is told.

defs: To exhaust of force or strength; to waste; to wear away; as, the violence of the waves was spent.
quotes: Their bodies spent with long labor and thirst.

defs: To expend money or any other possession; to consume, use, waste, or part with, anything; as, he who gets easily spends freely.
quotes: He spends as a person who knows that he must come to a reckoning.

defs: To waste or wear away; to be consumed; to lose force or strength; to vanish; as, energy spends in the using of it.
quotes: The sound spendeth and is dissipated in the open air.
The vines that they use for wine are so often cut, that their sap spendeth into the grapes.

defs: To break ground; to continue working.
